page.content: In the period from 2019 to 2024, Oracle's user engagement metrics reveal significant growth trends.

lines.text: In the period from 2019 to 2024, Oracle's user engagement metrics reveal significant growth trends.

page.content:
In 2019, active users totaled 1,200,000 with a user growth rate of 15%, and an average session duration of 5.2 minutes. This growth continued in 2020 with 1,500,000 active users, leading to an increase to 1,800,000 in 2021, reflecting a 20% growth rate and an average session duration of 6.1 minutes. By 2022, active users reached 2,100,000, with a 25% growth rate and an average session duration of 6.8 minutes, culminating in 2023 with 2,500,000 active users and a projected 2024 total of 3,000,000. The total users stand at 3,000,000, with user retention at 70%, new users comprising 900,000, and returning users at 2,100,000, resulting in a user churn rate of 5%. Monthly active users are 300,000, with user satisfaction at 85%, an average revenue per user of HKD120, total revenue of HKD360M, market share at 15%, and a global ranking in the top 5.

In terms of customer acquisition, in 2019, units sold were 12,000 with a customer count of 5,000 and an acquisition cost of HKD2000, while marketing spend was HKD10M with a sales team size of 25 and a retention rate of 80%. This evolved to 15,000 units sold in 2020, 18,000 in 2021, 20,000 in 2022, 22,000 in 2023, and a target of 25,000 in 2024, with respective customer counts of 6,000, 7,000, 8,000, 9,000, and 10,000, decreasing acquisition costs from HKD1800 to HKD1300, with conversion rates increasing from 15% to 20%, marketing spend rising from HKD10M to HKD25M, and sales team size expanding from 25 to 50, while retention rates improved from 80% to 92%.
